idea-plugin.name: JSON Notes
idea-plugin.description:
JSON Notes for all JetBrains IDEs — format, minify, and visualize JSON with an interactive tree view. Keep multiple JSON snippets in persistent, synced tabs.
  • Notes-style tabs — multiple document tabs; work with several JSONs side by side; names and content persist
  • Cross-IDE — tabs shared across IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, Rider, and other JetBrains IDEs
  • Settings Sync — tabs sync across machines via JetBrains Settings Sync
  • Format & minify — pretty-print or remove whitespace; works on any text, preserves strings
  • Tree view — interactive tree with color-coded types; expand/collapse all; copy value from context menu
  • Property grid — inspect selected node key/value or children in a side panel
  • Search — Cmd+F / Ctrl+F with next/previous and highlights in both text and tree
  • Paste / Copy — quick clipboard actions in the toolbar
  • Main editor — open JSON Notes in the editor area (Tools menu)
  • Keyboard shortcuts — optional keybindings for new tab, open with new tab, and editor actions; shortcut reference in JSON Notes settings
  • Plain text — optional keyword highlighting for common programming tokens; word-occurrence highlights in the text editor

Inspired by Online JSON Viewer (jsonviewer.stack.hu).
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.5 (2026-03-25)

  • Plain text — optional keyword highlighting for common programming keywords (colors per language family); toggle in JSON Notes settings.
  • Text editor — word occurrence highlighting for the word at the caret or selection (debounced updates).
  • Layout — refined toolbar and viewer spacing; optional side toolbar; consistent chrome and borders.
  • Tabs & focus — tab shortcuts avoid stealing focus from editable text and the notes search field; improved next/previous tab behavior.

1.0.4 (2026-03-23)

  • Keyboard shortcuts — default bindings where applicable: Open JSON Notes with New Tab (Ctrl+Shift+Alt+N), New JSON Notes Tab (Ctrl+Shift+Alt+T), Open JSON Notes in Editor (Ctrl+Shift+Alt+J), Open in Editor with New Tab on macOS (⌥⌘J). Next / Previous tab actions are in Keymap (search "JSON Notes") with no default shortcut.
  • Actions — new tab, next/previous tab, open with new tab, and open editor with new tab work from the active JSON Notes panel (tool window or main editor).
  • Settings — "Keyboard shortcuts" section lists JSON Notes actions and shows the current shortcuts from your active keymap; link text points to Settings → Keymap.
  • Keymap — all JSON Notes actions are grouped under JSON Notes for search and rebinding.

1.0.3 (2026-03-22)

  • Main editor — Tools → Open JSON Notes in Editor (Ctrl+Shift+Alt+J, ⌃⇧⌥J on macOS); Undo / Redo fixed with a writable document; full TextEditor integration and shared document between the tool window and editor
  • Tabs — confirmation before closing a tab; clearer tab bar and navigation
  • Search — debounced find with highlights in text and tree
  • Appearance — font family/size in settings; optional hiding of toolbar actions; layout and hover polish
  • Stability — safer document/tree access on background threads; safer text updates and caret handling when content changes

1.0.1 and earlier

  • Format, minify, and view JSON in a dedicated tool window
  • Multiple tabs with cross-IDE persistence and Settings Sync
  • Interactive tree view, search, and property grid

action.description: Open the JSON Notes tool window

action.text: Open JSON Notes with New Tab
action.description: Show the JSON Notes tool window and create a new tab there
Create a new tab in JSON Notes (tool window or main editor)
Switch to the next JSON Notes tab
Switch to the previous JSON Notes tab
Open JSON Notes in the main editor area (same features as the tool window)

action.text: Open JSON Notes in Editor with New Tab
action.description: Open JSON Notes in the main editor area and create a new tab
